refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from mission context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Atomicity requires separating these concerns. CR-NGHE-005.1 focuses on the derivation activity—the logical transformation from mission inputs to performance targets—which is the core functional obligation. Recording and management are separate concerns that belong in distinct sub-FRs. The refined statement remains solution-neutral by specifying 'derive' (a functional verb) without prescribing the mechanism (e.g., algorithm, tool, manual process).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that given a defined worksite mission context (inputs: site constraints, equipment type, production goals, safety policies), the system produces a documented set of performance targets (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that are traceable to those inputs and satisfy the acceptance criterion of documented, traceable performance targets.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements with traceability to worksite mission inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement baselines and calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall manage performance requirement changes in accordance with worksite operational policies.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Establish a parameterized mission-context intake process that captures worksite operational profile (hazard class, duty cycle, environmental conditions, safety constraints) and maps each input to a corresponding performance target via a documented traceability matrix, with version control for baseline snapshots.

DP-B: Implement a rule-based inference engine that translates worksite safety plan directives and operational policies into quantified performance constraints, storing results in a structured requirement specification with audit trail.

DP-C: Design a two-stage derivation workflow: (1) extract mission context from worksite documentation (safety plan, operational procedures, hazard assessments); (2) apply a standardized performance-mapping template to convert each context element into measurable targets, with manual review gates and sign-off checkpoints.

DP-D: Create a federated data model where worksite mission context is stored as immutable input records, performance targets are computed via a stateless transformation service, and all derivations are logged with timestamp, actor, and rationale for full traceability and historical reconstruction.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Create a federated data model where worksite mission context is stored as immutable input records, performance targets are computed via a stateless transformation service, and all derivations are logged with timestamp, actor, and rationale for full traceability and historical reconstruction.

potential_sources: Dependency on external worksite documentation formats (safety plan, operational policies) — mitigated by defining a canonical intake schema that normalizes heterogeneous inputs.
Coupling between immutable context storage and transformation service — acceptable because the service is stateless and can be versioned independently; context records are append-only.
Audit trail completeness depends on consistent logging discipline — risk is operational, not architectural; recommend logging middleware to enforce capture.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D avoids tight coupling by separating concerns: context capture (input), derivation logic (transformation), and traceability (audit log). Each can evolve independently. The immutable-record pattern supports historical baseline reconstruction and calibration audits without re-computation. Stateless transformation allows algorithm updates without invalidating prior results.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in documented form (safety plan, operational policies) at system initialization. RISK: If documentation is incomplete or informal, intake process may fail or require manual interpretation. MITIGATION: Define minimum required context elements and provide guided intake templates.

ASSUMPTION: Performance-target derivation rules are deterministic and can be codified. RISK: If rules are subjective or domain-expert-dependent, transformation service may not capture all nuance. MITIGATION: Embed domain expert review gates in the workflow; allow manual override with rationale logging.

ASSUMPTION: Immutable context records and audit logs can be stored and queried efficiently at scale. RISK: If worksite generates high-frequency context updates or large historical datasets, storage and retrieval latency may impact usability. MITIGATION: Implement tiered storage (hot/cold) and define retention policies.

ASSUMPTION: Traceability matrix and baseline history are sufficient for regulatory/safety compliance. RISK: If compliance requires real-time derivation or continuous re-validation, the batch-oriented approach may be inadequate. MITIGATION: Clarify compliance requirements early; consider event-driven re-derivation if needed.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are stable during a performance-target baseline period. RISK: If policies change frequently, baseline validity may degrade. MITIGATION: Define baseline validity windows and trigger re-derivation on policy change events.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR-NGHE-005.1 is well-formed and maps cleanly to DP-D. Recommend clarifying acceptance criteria: (1) Define 'documented set of performance targets' — specify format (e.g., JSON schema, spreadsheet, requirements database). (2) Define 'traceable to worksite inputs' — specify minimum traceability granularity (e.g., each target linked to at least one context input). (3) Define 'history of baselines/calibration updates' — specify retention period, query interface, and audit log schema. (4) Clarify whether performance targets are derived once at system initialization or re-derived on policy/context changes. These clarifications will inform IM (Implementation Module) design and acceptance testing strategy.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Federated data model layer encompassing immutable mission context records, stateless performance target computation service, and comprehensive derivation logging with traceability metadata (timestamp, actor, rationale)

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingest worksite mission context and performance telemetry as immutable input records

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Emit computed performance targets and derived task parameters to downstream consumers

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Publish safety-critical derivations and constraint violations with full traceability context

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Synchronize federated data model state and derivation logs across distributed nodes for consistency

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit: NGHE Solution is the logical home for mission-level data modeling, stateless transformation services, and audit-trail logging. Immutable records and traceability are core to NGHE governance. Minor diffusion: performance target consumption by Vehicle and Teleoperation requires outbound Tasking interface, but derivation logic remains centralized.

Poor fit: Vehicle is a consumer of performance targets, not a producer of the federated model. Placing the data model here would scatter mission context logic across vehicle instances, breaking centralized traceability and immutability guarantees. High diffusion of derivation logic.

Poor fit: Teleoperation is an operational interface layer, not a data governance layer. Embedding the federated model here conflates user interaction with authoritative data derivation. Traceability and actor attribution would diffuse across multiple teleoperation sessions.

Moderate fit: Safety Services could host derivation logging for safety-critical targets, but the DP explicitly requires a general-purpose federated model for all mission context and performance targets, not just safety constraints. Placing the entire model here would artificially narrow scope and diffuse non-safety derivations elsewhere.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the authoritative owner of the federated data model because: (1) it is the system-level integration layer responsible for mission orchestration and context management; (2) immutable records and stateless transformations align with NGHE's role as a trusted, auditable decision-support system; (3) centralized logging with timestamp, actor, and rationale is a governance function best housed at the solution level, not distributed across vehicles or teleoperation clients; (4) the Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces naturally connect the model to consumers and sources without requiring the model to migrate into lower-level modules.
containment_risks: Risk: If performance target computation becomes computationally intensive, latency-sensitive consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation) may experience delays. Mitigation: Implement caching and edge-side derivation caches that reference the authoritative NGHE model via Network Link.

Risk: Federated data model synchronization across distributed nodes (via Network Link) may introduce consistency windows. Mitigation: Define explicit consistency guarantees (eventual vs. strong) and version immutable records with logical timestamps.

Risk: Actor attribution in derivation logs requires integration with identity/authorization systems not explicitly listed. Mitigation: Assume actor context is injected via Tasking or SafetyAlert interface metadata.
Risk: Historical reconstruction queries may become expensive if the derivation log grows unbounded. Mitigation: Implement log retention policies and archival strategies within NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. Acceptance criteria explicitly require documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained history of baselines/calibration updates. The DP's immutable input records, stateless transformation service, and comprehensive derivation logging with timestamp/actor/rationale directly satisfy these requirements. Verification can be performed by: (1) auditing immutable record ingestion from Telemetry interface against worksite mission context; (2) validating derivation logs contain required metadata; (3) reconstructing historical baselines from log entries. All are testable and observable within NGHE Solution boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: EXCELLENT. The NGHE Solution module is unambiguously the authoritative owner. The DP's core elements (federated data model, stateless transformation, traceability logging) are system-level governance functions that must be centralized to ensure immutability and auditability. Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns derivation logic and audit trail; Vehicle and Teleoperation are consumers via Tasking interface; Safety Services receives safety-critical derivations via SafetyAlert interface. No ambiguity in ownership boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly and completely addresses FR-NGHE-005.1. The requirement is to 'derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.' The DP specifies: (1) immutable input records for mission context (source); (2) stateless transformation service (derivation mechanism); (3) computed performance targets (output); (4) full traceability with timestamp, actor, rationale (auditability). The DP is solution-complete and logically sound. No functional gaps identified.

diffusion_risks: MODERATE: Performance target computation latency. If derivation service becomes a bottleneck, consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation) may experience delays. Mitigation strategy exists (edge-side caching via Network Link), but requires explicit SLA definition and cache invalidation policy to prevent stale targets. Recommend adding latency acceptance criteria to DP.

MODERATE: Federated data model synchronization consistency. Network Link interface enables distributed state replication, but consistency windows are not explicitly defined. Risk: consumers may operate on divergent versions of mission context. Mitigation: Define explicit consistency model (eventual vs. strong consistency) and version immutable records with logical timestamps. Recommend adding consistency guarantees to DP specification.

LOW: Actor attribution dependency. Derivation logs require actor context (who initiated the derivation). This depends on integration with identity/authorization systems not explicitly owned by NGHE Solution. Risk: actor field may be missing or incorrect if upstream systems fail to inject context. Mitigation: Assume actor context is injected via Tasking or SafetyAlert interface metadata; add interface contract validation. Recommend documenting actor context injection requirements.

LOW: Unbounded derivation log growth. Historical reconstruction queries may degrade if log grows without bounds. Risk: storage and query performance degradation over time. Mitigation: Implement log retention policies and archival strategies within NGHE Solution. Recommend adding log lifecycle management to DP.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: EXCELLENT containment. The NGHE Solution module achieves 0.92 containment score with only 0.08 diffusion. The DP is tightly bound to NGHE's role as system-level mission orchestrator and trusted decision-support layer. All core elements (immutable records, stateless transformation, traceability logging) are naturally housed within NGHE Solution. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cleanly separate data sources, consumers, and synchronization concerns without requiring the DP to scatter across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services modules. The minor diffusion (0.08) reflects only the necessary outbound Tasking interface to consumers—a healthy architectural boundary, not a containment failure. No evidence of logic duplication, scattered responsibilities, or interface confusion. Recommend proceeding with implementation.

recommended_revisions: ADD LATENCY SLA: Specify maximum acceptable latency for performance target derivation and emission via Tasking interface. Define edge-side caching strategy and cache invalidation policy to prevent stale targets reaching Vehicle/Teleoperation consumers.

ADD CONSISTENCY MODEL: Explicitly define consistency guarantees for federated data model synchronization across distributed nodes via Network Link. Specify whether eventual or strong consistency is required, and document version/timestamp strategy for immutable records.

ADD ACTOR CONTEXT CONTRACT: Document the interface contract for actor attribution in derivation logs. Specify which upstream systems (identity/authorization) must inject actor context, and define validation rules for missing or malformed actor fields.

ADD LOG LIFECYCLE POLICY: Define retention period, archival strategy, and query performance targets for derivation logs. Specify how historical reconstruction queries will be optimized (e.g., indexed by timestamp, actor, rationale) to prevent unbounded growth from degrading system performance.

ADD WORKSITE SAFETY PLAN INTEGRATION: Explicitly document how worksite safety plan and operational policies (listed in constraints) are ingested as immutable input records via Telemetry interface. Specify validation rules to ensure performance targets respect safety constraints.

CLARIFY EDGE-SIDE CACHING ARCHITECTURE: If edge-side derivation caches are implemented (per containment risk mitigation), document their relationship to the authoritative NGHE model. Specify cache coherency protocol and fallback behavior if Network Link synchronization fails.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence, and containment is excellent (0.92). However, the recommended_revisions list identifies six critical specification gaps that must be addressed before proceeding to sub-FR decomposition. Given the HIGH safety criticality of this requirement (performance targets directly influence vehicle behavior and safety-critical decisions), these gaps pose unacceptable risk if left unresolved at the current level. Specifically: (1) latency SLA and cache invalidation policy are essential to prevent stale safety-critical targets from reaching Vehicle/Teleoperation; (2) consistency model and versioning strategy are mandatory for federated data synchronization in safety-critical contexts; (3) actor context contract and log lifecycle policy ensure auditability and traceability required by acceptance criteria; (4) worksite safety plan integration must be explicit to guarantee performance targets respect operational constraints; (5) edge-side caching architecture clarification is needed to maintain coherency with authoritative NGHE model. These are not minor enhancements—they are foundational to the DP's correctness and safety assurance. Revising the DP now to incorporate these specifications will prevent downstream rework and ensure sub-FRs are derived from a complete, safety-assured parent requirement.

revise.why: The DP (FR-NGHE-005.1 Design Parameter) must be revised to incorporate all six recommended revisions before sub-FR decomposition proceeds. Specifically: (1) Add explicit latency SLA (e.g., 'derivation service shall emit performance targets to Tasking interface within X ms of mission context update') and define edge-side cache invalidation policy (e.g., 'cache entries expire after Y seconds or upon explicit invalidation signal from NGHE Solution'); (2) Add consistency model specification (e.g., 'federated data model shall maintain eventual consistency with maximum divergence window of Z seconds, enforced via logical timestamps on immutable records'); (3) Add actor context injection contract (e.g., 'Tasking interface shall include actor identity in request metadata; NGHE Solution shall validate actor field presence and log derivation with attributed actor or reject request if actor context is missing'); (4) Add log lifecycle policy (e.g., 'derivation logs shall be retained for minimum N days, archived to cold storage after M days, indexed by timestamp/actor/rationale for query performance'); (5) Add worksite safety plan integration specification (e.g., 'Telemetry interface shall ingest worksite safety plan and operational policies as immutable records; derivation service shall validate computed performance targets against safety constraints before emission'); (6) Add edge-side caching architecture clarification (e.g., 'edge-side caches are read-only replicas of authoritative NGHE model; cache coherency is maintained via Network Link synchronization with version vectors; fallback behavior on Network Link failure is to use last-known-good cached targets with explicit staleness warning to consumers'). These revisions transform the DP from logically sound but under-specified into a complete, safety-assured design parameter ready for sub-FR decomposition.

assumptions: Latency SLA for performance target derivation and emission is acceptable to Vehicle and Teleoperation consumers (assumed <500ms based on typical mission planning cycles, but must be validated with stakeholders).

Federated data model synchronization via Network Link can achieve eventual consistency within acceptable divergence windows (assumed <10 seconds for non-safety-critical targets, <1 second for safety-critical targets, but must be validated with network architecture team).

Actor context is reliably injected by upstream identity/authorization systems via Tasking interface metadata (assumed available from existing NGHE authentication layer, but must be validated with security team).

Derivation log retention and archival policies are compatible with operational data governance requirements (assumed 90-day hot retention, 1-year cold archival, but must be validated with data governance stakeholders).

Worksite safety plan and operational policies are available as structured data via Telemetry interface (assumed provided by worksite mission planning system, but must be validated with mission planning team).

Edge-side caching is optional optimization, not mandatory for correctness (assumed caching is performance enhancement only, with fallback to authoritative NGHE model, but must be validated with performance requirements).
open_risks: SAFETY-CRITICAL: If latency SLA is not met, stale performance targets may reach Vehicle/Teleoperation, causing incorrect mission execution or safety violations. Mitigation: Define explicit latency acceptance criteria and implement monitoring/alerting for SLA violations.

SAFETY-CRITICAL: If consistency model is not defined, distributed consumers may operate on divergent versions of mission context, leading to inconsistent performance target interpretation. Mitigation: Define explicit consistency guarantees (eventual vs. strong) and implement version/timestamp strategy for immutable records.

SAFETY-CRITICAL: If worksite safety plan integration is not explicit, performance targets may violate operational constraints, causing safety incidents. Mitigation: Add explicit validation rules to ensure performance targets respect safety constraints before emission.

MODERATE: If actor context injection fails, derivation logs will lack attribution, breaking auditability and traceability requirements. Mitigation: Implement interface contract validation and reject derivations with missing actor context.

MODERATE: If log lifecycle policy is not defined, unbounded log growth may degrade query performance and storage capacity over time. Mitigation: Implement retention policies, archival strategies, and query optimization (indexing).

MODERATE: If edge-side caching architecture is not clarified, cache coherency failures may cause consumers to operate on stale targets without awareness. Mitigation: Document cache coherency protocol and fallback behavior on Network Link synchronization failure.
evidence_needed: Latency SLA validation: Stakeholder confirmation that proposed latency target (e.g., <500ms) is acceptable to Vehicle and Teleoperation consumers; network architecture analysis confirming Network Link can support SLA; performance testing of derivation service under peak load.

Consistency model validation: Distributed systems analysis confirming eventual consistency model is sufficient for mission context synchronization; network architecture analysis confirming logical timestamp strategy is feasible; testing of version vector implementation for immutable records.

Actor context contract validation: Security team confirmation that identity/authorization systems reliably inject actor context via Tasking interface; interface specification documenting actor field schema and validation rules; testing of actor context injection and log attribution.

Log lifecycle policy validation: Data governance stakeholder confirmation that retention/archival policies align with operational requirements; storage capacity analysis confirming archival strategy is feasible; query performance testing confirming indexed log queries meet performance targets.

Worksite safety plan integration validation: Mission planning team confirmation that safety plan and operational policies are available as structured data via Telemetry interface; specification of safety constraint validation rules; testing of performance target validation against safety constraints.

Edge-side caching architecture validation: Performance analysis confirming caching provides measurable latency improvement; network architecture analysis confirming cache coherency protocol is feasible; testing of fallback behavior on Network Link synchronization failure.
